data.title: Paramount’s bid for Warner Bros. clears U.S. antitrust waiting period
data.description:
• Paramount Skydance (PSKY) disclosed in an SEC filing on Friday that its antitrust waiting period of 10 days for its all-cash offer to buy Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) has expired in the U.S. 
  • The company said the waiting period expired on February 19, 2026, at 11:59 p.m., Eastern Time.
  • The waiting period expired following Paramount’s certification of compliance with the DoJ's second request for information on December 23, 2025.
  • "The expiration of the HSR waiting period means there is no statutory impediment in the U.S. to closing Paramount’s proposed acquisition of WBD," the company said.
  • Paramount said it continues to engage constructively with antitrust enforcers and other regulators globally.
  • The company said it secured clearance for the WBD deal from the foreign investment authorities in Germany on January 27, 2026.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: CTO Realty Growth declares $0.38 dividend
data.description:

data.title: TriSalus prices $40 mln public stock offering
data.description:
• TriSalus Life Sciences (TLSI) said on Friday it priced its underwritten public offering of 9,756,100 shares of common stock at $4.10 per share.
  • The company expects gross proceeds of about $40.0 million before underwriting discounts, commissions, and other expenses.
  • TriSalus has granted the underwriter a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1,463,415 shares, representing 15% of the shares sold in the offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 23, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions. 
  • TLSI -7.15% premarket to $4.28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump says shutdown cut “at least two points in GDP” ahead of fourth quarter read
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said the recent government shutdown cost the economy “at least two points in GDP,” according to a post on Truth Social ahead of Friday’s fourth-quarter GDP data release.

Trump appeared to be referring to the 43-day shutdown that began in October. His comments come as investors await official data that could show how much the disruption weighed on economic growth in the final quarter of the year.

Analysts expect the U.S. economy to have expanded at a 3% annualized pace in the fourth quarter, slowing from 4.4% growth in the previous quarter. Economists have said the prolonged government shutdown likely weighed on activity during the period, contributing to the expected deceleration in growth.

data.title: Upstart reports $333M sale of Upstart Auto assets to Bayview
data.description:
AI lending marketplace Upstart (UPST) announced the sale of a $333M aggregate portfolio of Upstart Auto assets to affiliates of Bayview Asset Management.

“Our exciting partnership with Bayview, who has both experience and scale in auto, validates our retail and refinance products momentum and comes at the perfect time as we continue expanding our auto business,” said Sanjay Datta, President and Chief Capital Officer at Upstart. “We look forward to continuing and growing our partnership with the team at Bayview.”

The auto portfolio sale demonstrates growing interest in Upstart’s scaling auto platforms as well as strategic funding to optimize balance sheet usage for its newer products.

Upstart (UPST) also announced separately a forward-flow commitment from funds managed by Wafra, pursuant to which Wafra has agreed to purchase up to $200M of assets originated through the Upstart auto finance platform.

The agreement consists of a 12-month forward flow arrangement and is the first such agreement between Upstart and Wafra.

“Wafra's investment will help Upstart to continue our goal of pursuing the best rates and best process to help borrowers with their auto financing needs,” the company said.

data.title: Lifeward to implement 1-for-12 reverse share split
data.description:
• Lifeward (Nasdaq: LFWD) on Friday it will implement a 1-for-12 reverse split of its ordinary shares effective at the open of market on February 24, 2026.
  • Under the reverse split, every 12 issued and outstanding ordinary shares will be combined into one share.
  • The company also said its board approved increasing authorized share capital to 100 million shares.
  • Lifeward said it expects the reverse split to help it meet Nasdaq’s continued listing standards, including the minimum $1.00 per share bid price requirement.
  • The company expects outstanding shares to decrease to about 1.5 million from approximately 18.3 million as a result of the split.
  • The shares will trade under a new CUSIP number beginning February 24, 2026.
  • LFWD +1.92% premarket to $0.53.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Western Alliance Bank provides $7.5 mln credit facility to OnQ
data.description:
• Western Alliance Bank (WAL) (WAL.PR.A) on Friday said it has provided OnQ with a $7.5 million asset-based line of credit through its Asset-Based Lending Group.
  • The facility includes a $7.5 million asset-based revolving line of credit and treasury management services to support the retail display company’s growth.
  • OnQ provides retail display and systems solutions to global brands and retailers, with installations in more than 10,000 stores worldwide.
  • WAL +0.33% after hours to $91.0532.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ICU Medical outlines 2026 EPS guidance of $7.75-$8.45 while targeting margin gains and debt reduction
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ICU Medical (ICUI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Vivek Jain, CEO & Chairman of the Board, highlighted that, "Revenue for Q4 was $536 million for total company growth of 2% on an organic basis or minus 14% reported, and we finished with 5% organic growth for the company for full year 2025." He noted record revenue quarters for both consumables and systems businesses, strong cash generation, and further net debt reduction to just below $1 billion. Jain emphasized, "Both our consumables and systems businesses delivered record revenue quarters operationally," and flagged the deconsolidation of IV solutions following the Otsuka ICU Medical JV as impacting reported results. He also reported completion of major integration milestones, including the closure of the FDA warning letter for Smiths Medical and manufacturing site integrations, stating, "We should have more strategic choices available to us." Jain confirmed ongoing FDA 510(k) pursuits for new pump lines and progress on ERP unification, and reiterated a focus on achieving long-term innovation and operational synergies.

  • Brian Bonnell, CFO & Treasurer, stated, "Adjusted gross margin for the fourth quarter was 40.5%, which was in line with the guidance we provided on the Q3 call of 40% to 41%." He noted, "Adjusted SG&A expense was $113 million in Q4 and adjusted R&D was $21 million," and that total adjusted operating expenses represented 25% of revenue. Bonnell added, "Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $98 million, a decrease of 7% from $106 million last year," attributing declines to IV Solutions deconsolidation and tariffs. He reported free cash flow of $44 million for the quarter and $100 million for the year, and outlined a continued priority on debt paydown, with a long-term leverage target of 2x.

Outlook

  • Bonnell provided 2026 guidance: "We expect full year 2026 consolidated organic revenue growth in the low to mid-single-digit range, and we expect the organic growth rates for each of the underlying business units to generally be in line with the longer-term outlook that we've provided the last several years, which is mid-single digits for both consumables and infusion systems and flat to down slightly for Vital Care." He projected adjusted gross margin at "around 41%" for 2026, with improvements throughout the year as integration projects complete. Guidance for adjusted operating expenses is "approximately 25% for 2026, consisting of 21% for SG&A and 4% for R&D." Bonnell stated, "2026 adjusted EBITDA range of $400 million to $430 million and adjusted EPS in the range of $7.75 to $8.45 per share."

  • Jain reinforced strategic priorities: "We believe these investments alongside good commercial execution will allow us to continue the revenue trends longer term." He also pointed to the completion of major integration work and innovation as drivers for sustainable growth.

Financial Results

  • ICU Medical reported Q4 revenue of $536 million and EBITDA of $98 million. Adjusted gross margin was 40.5%. Adjusted SG&A expense was $113 million, adjusted R&D $21 million, and total adjusted operating expenses $134 million (25% of revenue). Adjusted diluted EPS for Q4 was $1.91 compared to $2.11 last year. Free cash flow was $44 million for Q4 and $100 million for the year. Debt at quarter-end was $1.3 billion, with $308 million cash on hand. The company paid down $30 million of principal in Q4, totaling $303 million for the year.

  • Tariff expense for Q4 was $11 million, and restructuring/integration expenses were $20 million. Net interest expense for Q4 was $18 million, and the adjusted effective tax rate was 23%.

Q&A

  • Jayson Bedford, Raymond James: Asked if customers are actively making decisions in the current capital environment. Jain replied, "the capital environment has been very stable, nothing different than historical behavior. Deals are getting done."

  • Bedford: Queried on the mix of LVP business. Jain stated, "Domestically, in the United States for the U.S. portion of our business, absolutely. Internationally, 60 continues to be placed."

  • Bedford: Asked about the impact of pending clearances on sales. Jain responded, "any customer who is serious about the platform is very interested in what the future road map looks like and wants to engage on it and it hasn't been slowing anything down."

  • Brett Fishbin, KeyBanc: Asked about consumables growth and hospital utilization. Jain said, "when we look at our underlying demand, we haven't seen any impact along the line of utilization on anything right now." On tariffs, Bonnell noted, "We have done some things structurally to try to mitigate the tariffs as much as we can."

  • Michael Matson, Needham: Questioned market size and share in syringe pumps. Jain explained, "the market size is much smaller. And it's the inverse of where we are in LVPs. Our share is actually higher on syringe... than we have in LVP."

  • Jason Bednar, Piper Sandler: Asked about system business outlook and OEM wind down. Jain noted confidence in backlog and installation schedule, adding, "that is a piece of business that has been declining for the last 2 years... and the business still did well."

  • Lawrence Solow, CJS Securities: Inquired about margin gains from consolidation. Jain confirmed, "once the inventory that was made at the old factory leaves, we get the benefit of the manufacturing synergization."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a neutral to slightly positive tone, with questions focused on business stability, growth drivers, and operational improvements, while pressing for more clarity on tariffs, integration benefits, and the impact of product cycles.

  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, emphasizing operational progress, innovation, and financial discipline, with Jain stating, "we feel like we've weathered those issues. All in all, it's a good place to be with our best businesses growing."

  • Compared to last quarter, both management and analysts exhibited greater confidence, with less skepticism around integration and more focus on execution and operational leverage.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company maintained its mid-single-digit growth guidance for consumables and systems, consistent with the prior quarter. Guidance language remained stable, but the Q4 call included more detailed projections for 2026 EBITDA ($400 million to $430 million) and EPS ($7.75 to $8.45).

  • Management's tone shifted to a more confident outlook, referencing completion of major integration projects and closure of the Smiths Medical FDA warning letter. Analysts' questions evolved from skepticism about integration and tariffs to inquiries about growth acceleration, margin expansion, and the timing of strategic initiatives.

  • Key metrics such as free cash flow and margin targets were reiterated, with an emphasis on annualizing cost savings and leveraging completed integrations.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited tariffs and the deconsolidation of IV Solutions as material headwinds. Bonnell noted that tariffs and the IV Solutions divestiture combined have a $25 million impact on EBITDA.

  • Ongoing integration and restructuring costs remain a challenge but are expected to decline as projects complete.

  • Analysts raised concerns about potential deceleration in hospital utilization, further tariff risk, and the complexity of extracting and optimizing less profitable business segments such as Vital Care.

Final Takeaway

ICU Medical’s fourth quarter call underscored strong execution on integration, innovation, and debt reduction, supporting a stable outlook for 2026 with projected EPS of $7.75 to $8.45 and improved free cash flow. Management emphasized the company’s readiness to capitalize on a modernized product portfolio and completed integration activities, while prioritizing further margin gains and deleveraging. Tariffs and the IV Solutions deconsolidation remain notable headwinds, but leadership sees these as manageable within the current operational trajectory, with core businesses positioned for record revenue in the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sernova to amend warrants, extend expiry, and cut exercise price
data.description:
• Sernova Biotherapeutics (SVA:CA) (OTC: SEOVF) said on Friday it plans to amend certain outstanding warrants, subject to approval from the TSX.
  • The company will extend the expiry date to March 3, 2027 for 19,977,050 common share purchase warrants originally issued in September 2024 at an exercise price of $0.30 and set to expire on March 3, 2026, which excludes warrants held by insiders.
  • Sernova will also amend 5,466,250 investor warrants and 105,000 finder warrants issued in October and November 2025 by reducing the exercise price to $0.25 from $0.40 and adding a provision allowing the company to accelerate expiry if the five-day volume-weighted average trading price exceeds $0.50.
  • No insider-held warrants will be amended.
  • The changes are set to take effect on March 6, 2026.
  • SEOVF closed -0.67% at $0.1033.
  • Source: Press Release
